title: Amendment I
content: Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion, or prohibiting the free exercise thereof; or abridging the freedom of speech, or of the press; or the right of the people peaceably to assemble, and to petition the Government for a redress of grievances.

title: Amendment II
content: A well regulated Militia, being necessary to the security of a free State, the right of the people to keep and bear Arms, shall not be infringed.

title: Amendment III
content: No Soldier shall, in time of peace be quartered in any house, without the consent of the Owner.

title: Amendment IV
content: The right of the people to be secure in their persons, houses, papers, and effects, against unreasonable searches and seizures...

title: Amendment V
content: No person shall be held to answer for a capital crime... nor shall be compelled in any criminal case to be a witness against himself...

title: Amendment VI
content: In all criminal prosecutions, the accused shall enjoy the right to a speedy and public trial...

title: Amendment VII
content: In Suits at common law... the right of trial by jury shall be preserved...

title: Amendment VIII
content: Excessive bail shall not be required, nor excessive fines imposed...

title: Amendment IX
content: The enumeration in the Constitution of certain rights shall not be construed to deny others retained by the people.

title: Amendment X
content: The powers not delegated to the United States... are reserved to the States respectively.

title: Amendment XIII
content: Neither slavery nor involuntary servitude... shall exist within the United States.

title: Amendment XIV
content: All persons born or naturalized in the United States... are citizens...

title: Amendment XV
content: The right of citizens to vote shall not be denied on account of race...

title: Amendment XIX
content: The right to vote shall not be denied on account of sex.

title: Amendment XXVI
content: The right to vote shall not be denied to citizens 18 or older.
